MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-30-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph May, Esq. 415-781-3333 for Plaintiff.
Ben Rothstein, Esq. 415-775-6666 of Keker Van Nest & Peters for Defendant BIG BILLY, INC..

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant Argues issues and provides a copy of the response to the Court. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT BIG BILLY, INC. DBA SLIM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Big Billy Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. There are triable disputes whether: 1) Big Billy acted negligently in hiring defendant Lloyd Brown to provide security services at the 2016 HSBF; 2) Lloyd Brown acted negligently in hiring and retaining defendants Jose Beltran and William Douglass; and 3) Mr. Brown's asserted negligence in hiring and retaining Mr. Beltran and Mr. Douglass was a substantial factor in causing plaintiff Shane Stevens' injuries. Liberally construing the evidence in favor of Mr. Stevens and drawing all reasonable inferences in favor of Mr. Stevens, a reasonable juror could conclude that: 1) Big Billy acted negligently by knowingly hiring an unlicensed PPO in violation of the requirement of Bus. & Prof. Code 7582.3 or by failing to conduct a sufficient inquiry whether Mr. Brown had the required license; 2) Mr. Brown acted negligently by failing to conduct a sufficient inquiry into the license status, training and qualifications of Mr. Beltran and Mr. Douglass and by failing to provide them with sufficient training regarding the use of force; and 3) Mr. Brown's failure to ensure that Mr. Beltran and Mr. Douglass had sufficient training and qualifications regarding use of force was a substantial factor in Mr. Beltran and Mr. Douglass using force on Mr. Stevens which caused Mr. Stevens' injuries.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Motion To Compel Non-Party Jon Luini To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Joseph S. May, Tel. No. 1-415-781-3333 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Charles J. McCurdy of Boornazian, Jensen & Garthe, Tel. No. 1-510-834-4350
appearing for the Defendant BS Billy Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Non-Party Jon Luini To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And For Monetary Sanctions - Plaintiff Shane Stevens' motion to compel non-party Jon Luini to comply with subpoena for production of business records is granted subject to a protective order covering the information produced by Mr. Luini and Mr. Stevens' request for monetary sanctions is denied. The parties appear to agree that the video that is sought by the subpoena is discoverable. The sole dispute pertains to whether the video should be produced subject to restrictions. Because the video may be subject to contractual rights and obligations of Mr. Luini, so as not to disturb or disrupt those contractual rights and obligations, any party receiving video and other information from Mr. Luini per the subpoena must only use that information for purposes of this lawsuit, must only provide it to persons who have a legitimate good faith need to review the information for purposes of this lawsuit, and must return the information to Mr. Luini at the conclusion of this lawsuit without any copies of the information or any variants of the information being retained by the receiving party. Because the positions taken by Mr. Luini and defendant Big Billy, Inc. were substantially justified, no monetary sanctions are awarded to Mr. Stevens. The ruling on this motion moots Big Billy's motion for protective order, so that motion noticed to be heard on October 22, 2018 at 9am is now off calendar. Mr. Luini will produce the video by October 11, 2018. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
